preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: Startups needing an ai-first, intent-aware design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes context for startups seeking an AI-first, intent-aware design system. Anchors the page by clarifying what MODE is and who it's for, giving the Validator audience immediate relevance signals before any persuasion begins.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators need trust signals early. Showing customer logos, testimonials, or authority markers from recognizable startups or tech leaders satisfies the Validator's instinct to verify legitimacy before engaging with deeper content.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Required for the decision funnel stage and critical for the Validator archetype. Concrete metrics, case study data, and measurable outcomes (e.g., adoption speed, design velocity improvements) give the proof-driven Validator the hard data they need to justify commitment.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required at the decision funnel stage. Presents pricing tiers and plan comparison so startups can evaluate fit against their budget and team size. Positioned after evidence so the Validator has already internalized the ROI before seeing cost.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Immediately precedes Recovery per structural rules. Delivers a clear, confident CTA to start a paid plan, reinforced by the proof and pricing context already established. Framed as a low-friction commitment step for startups ready to act.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Always last. Provides secondary paths — contact sales, explore docs, or sign up for a free trial — catching Validators who need one more touchpoint before converting, plus standard footer navigation.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes context for startups seeking an AI-first, intent-aware design system. Anchors the page by clarifying what MODE is and who it's for, giving the Validator audience immediate relevance signals before any persuasion begins.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validators need trust signals early. Showing customer logos, testimonials, or authority markers from recognizable startups or tech leaders satisfies the Validator's instinct to verify legitimacy before engaging with deeper content.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: Required for the decision funnel stage and critical for the Validator archetype. Concrete metrics, case study data, and measurable outcomes (e.g., adoption speed, design velocity improvements) give the proof-driven Validator the hard data they need to justify commitment.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Required at the decision funnel stage. Presents pricing tiers and plan comparison so startups can evaluate fit against their budget and team size. Positioned after evidence so the Validator has already internalized the ROI before seeing cost.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: Immediately precedes Recovery per structural rules. Delivers a clear, confident CTA to start a paid plan, reinforced by the proof and pricing context already established. Framed as a low-friction commitment step for startups ready to act.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision stage funnel requires conversion focus. Per notes: 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only.' The persistent CTA in nav is essential for conversion optimization.

Decision stage requires price anchoring before commitment. Per notes: 'with-price for decision stage: centered offer presentation with price_display slot surfacing the price as a fact before the CTA.' Validator archetype always includes trust_signal.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: MODE is the semantic intelligence layer for code-first SaaS teams. Components that resolve behavior by archetype, funnel stage, and intent — without manual variants or QA overhead.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires credible attribution. Per notes: 'Always include role and company for Validator credibility' and 'with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity.' This is the primary trust signal for Validator archetype.

slots.quote: We cut our variant QA cycle from three days to under two hours. MODE makes the right call by default — we stopped hand-coding audience logic into every component after the first week.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype demands trust through attribution. Per notes: 'Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust.'

slots.headline: Measured outcomes from teams that have shipped with MODE.

stats.label: Median time to fully operational intelligence layer
Faster page architecture from brief to rendered output
Reduction in manual variant configuration reported by early adopters

page.reasoning: Decision stage conversion requires clear recommendation. Per notes: 'highlighted variant for recommended plan — use sparingly (one per pricing section).' Validator variant includes description and longer feature list to support informed decision-making.

slots.description: The full intelligence layer. Resolution engine, component manifest, IA planner, multi-variant generation, and Next.js preview UI. No subscription. No lock-in. Lives in your codebase.
features: Semantic resolution engine — archetype, funnel stage, intent state, context mode
Component manifest — 11 components, fully specified with slot schemas
IA planner + multi-variant generation powered by Claude Opus
Three palette presets — funnel-driven, archetype-driven, feature-emphasis
Token dashboard with editable palette map and accent config
Bring your own Anthropic API key — you own the token spend

page.reasoning: Decision stage requires focused conversion without distraction. Per notes: 'minimal for high-frequency use — above footer, between sections.' Validator variant includes trust_signal like 'No credit card required' to reduce friction at conversion moment.

slots.headline: The intelligence layer is ready when you are.
slots.subhead: Teams running MODE report their design system making the right call without intervention. One purchase, your codebase, no dependencies.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No credit card stored after checkout.

page.reasoning: Decision stage funnel on conversion page requires recovery CTA. Per notes: 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.'
